1.gl: sacred (physically, pure, morally blameless or religious, ceremonially, consecrated)

3.gl: glory (as very apparent), in a wide application (literal or figurative, objective or subjective)

4.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
4.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

5.gl: a slave (literal or figurative, involuntary or voluntary; frequently, therefore in a qualified sense of subjection or subserviency)

6.dv: from a primary (to anticipate, usually with pleasure);

7.gl: sacred (physically, pure, morally blameless or religious, ceremonially, consecrated)

10.gl: the heart, i.e. (figuratively) the thoughts or feelings (mind); also (by analogy) the middle

12.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
12.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

12.gl: the heart, i.e. (figuratively) the thoughts or feelings (mind); also (by analogy) the middle
